sections.heading: Considerations
sections.body:
To register with and log in to Atlas, you can use one -- and only
one -- of the following options:

- Your email account

- Your GitHub account

- Your Google account

> **Note:** If your company uses Federated Authentication,
> you must use your company email address.

sections.heading: GitHub Accounts
sections.body:
If you register with your GitHub Account, the following differences
apply when using your GitHub account with Atlas:

- GitHub manages your user details, email address, and password. You
  can't change this information using the Atlas console or API (Application Programming Interface).

- GitHub manages your 2FA (Two Factor Authentication). You can't use Atlas multi-factor
  authentication and won't be prompted for an Atlas 2FA (Two Factor Authentication) verification
  when you log into Atlas. GitHub should verify your identity using
  [GitHub 2-Step Verification](https://docs.github.com/en/authentication/securing-your-account-with-two-factor-authentication-2fa).

- If you [linked](atlas-register-account) your
  Atlas account to your GitHub Account, you can
  [unlink these accounts](unlink-your-mongodb-account-from-github)
  in Atlas.

sections.heading: Google Accounts
sections.body:
If you register with your Google Account, the following differences
apply when using your Google account with Atlas:

- Google manages your user details, email address, and password. You
  can't change this information using the Atlas console or API (Application Programming Interface).

- Google manages your 2FA (Two Factor Authentication). You can't use Atlas multi-factor
  authentication and won't be prompted for an Atlas 2FA (Two Factor Authentication) verification
  when you log into Atlas. Google should verify your identity using
  [Google 2-Step Verification](https://www.google.com/landing/2step/).

- If you [linked](atlas-register-account) your
  Atlas account to your Google Account, you can
  [unlink these accounts](unlink-your-mongodb-account-from-google)
  in Atlas.

sections.heading: MongoDB Cloud Manager Users
sections.body:
If you use MongoDB Cloud Manager, use your Cloud Manager credentials to
[log in](atlas-account-login) to Atlas. You can then
create a new Atlas project from Cloud Manager.

sections.heading: Add the Atlas CDN Host to your Allow List
sections.body:
Atlas uses a CDN (Content Delivery Network) to serve content quickly. If your organization
uses a firewall, add the following Atlas CDN (Content Delivery Network) host to the
firewall's allow list to prevent issues accessing the
Atlas UI: `https://assets.mongodb-cdn.com/`.

sections.heading: GitHub Accounts Require a Public Email Address
sections.body:
If you try to register with Github, you must have an public email
address associated with your GitHub account. Atlas returns an error
if you attempt to register without a public email address.

To set a public GitHub email address:

- After you log into GitHub, click **Settings** on your
  profile menu. Your **Public Profile** page should display.

- Select one email address from the **Public email** dropdown
  in your **Public Profile**.

  If GitHub greys out this dropdown menu:

  - Click **Emails** from the left-side menu.

  - Clear the **Keep my email addresses private** box.

  - Return to the **Public Profile** page and select an email
    address from the **Public email** dropdown.

- Click **Update profile** to save the changes.

sections.heading: Register a new Atlas Account
sections.body:
Select a tab based on how you would like to register your account:

Atlas CLI

To register with Atlas using the
Atlas CLI, run the following command:

```sh

atlas auth register [options]

```

To learn more about the command syntax and parameters, see the
Atlas CLI documentation for [atlas auth register](atlas/cli/current/command/atlas-auth-register).

Atlas UI

Email Address

- Provide the following information about yourself:

  - **Email Address**

  - **First Name**

  - **Last Name**

  - **Password**

    > **Note:** 
    >
    > Atlas
    >
    >  Password Policy
    >
    > Your Atlas **user account** password must:
    >
    > - Contain at least eight characters.
    >
    > - Exclude your email address and Atlas username.
    >
    > - Differ from your previous twenty four passwords.
    >
    > - Differ from the most commonly used passwords.

  - (Optional) **Company Name**

- Review the
  [Terms of Service](https://www.mongodb.com/cloud-terms-and-conditions?tck=docs) and
  the [Privacy Policy](https://www.mongodb.com/legal/privacy-policy?tck=docs_atlas).

- Click the checkbox to accept the Terms and Policy.

- Verify that you're not a robot.

- Click **Sign up**.

GitHub Account

- Click **GitHub**.

- Follow the GitHub prompts to sign in.

- Review the **Terms of Service** and the
  **Privacy Policy**.

- Select the checkbox to accept the
  **Terms of Service** and the
  **Privacy Policy**.

- Click **Submit**.

Google Account

- Click **Google**.

- Follow the Google prompts to sign in.

- Review the **Terms of Service** and the
  **Privacy Policy**.

- Select the checkbox to accept the
  **Terms of Service** and the
  **Privacy Policy**.

- Click **Submit**.

sections.heading: Register a new Atlas Account
sections.body:
> **Note:** If you run `atlas setup` with the default selections, you
> don't need to run `atlas auth register`.

sections.heading: Register a new Atlas Account
sections.body:
To register using the Atlas UI, start at the
MongoDB Atlas registration page.

sections.heading: Log in to Your Atlas Account
sections.body:
Select a tab based on how you would like to log in:

Atlas CLI

To authenticate with Atlas using the
Atlas CLI, run the following command:

```sh

atlas auth login [options]

```

To learn more about the command syntax and parameters, see the
Atlas CLI documentation for [atlas auth login](atlas/cli/current/command/atlas-auth-login).

Atlas UI

Email Address

If you already created a MongoDB account using your email
address, sign in to your account:

- Navigate to MongoDB Atlas login page.

- Enter your email address.

- Click **Next**.

- Follow the prompts to log in to your account.

GitHub Account

If you already created a MongoDB account using your GitHub
account, sign in to your GitHub account:

- Navigate to MongoDB Atlas login page.

- Click **GitHub**.

- Follow the prompts to log in using your GitHub account.

Google Account

If you already created a MongoDB account using your Google
account, sign in to your Google account:

- Navigate to MongoDB Atlas login page.

- Click **Google**.

- Follow the prompts to log in using your Google account.

sections.heading: Log in to Your Atlas Account
sections.body:
For step-by-step instructions on logging in using the
Atlas CLI, see
[Connect from the Atlas CLI](atlas/cli/stable/connect-atlas-cli).

sections.heading: Log in to Your Atlas Account
sections.body: To log in using the Atlas UI:

sections.heading: Create an Atlas Organization and Project
sections.body:
Atlas creates an organization and project for you. You can
also create them on your own.

At the Organization level, you
can implement security controls and create users which work across one
or more Projects. To learn more, see [The Organization Level](org-level-best-practices).

You can create project-level Atlas
users and roles with appropriate
access to the different production and development application
environments. To learn more, see [The Project Level](project-level-best-practices).

[Create an Atlas organization](create-organization) and
then [create a project](create-project) in this organization.
You will deploy your first cluster in this project.

sections.heading: Next Steps
sections.body:
With your Atlas account, open the organization and its project, and
then proceed to Deploy a Free Cluster.

> **See Also:** To learn about shortcuts you can use to navigate your new Atlas
> account, see [Quickly Navigate with Atlas Goto](atlas-go-to).
